<insights>

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
- The presence of context.md and llms.txt at the root suggests sourcevision has been run on the rex package itself, producing AI-readable summaries alongside human-readable docs.
- No entry points means this zone is purely declarative — build tooling and documentation only, which is the expected structure for a package root.
- Rex-F.png and Rex.png are brand assets that could be moved to a dedicated docs/ or assets/ directory to keep the root clean.
- context.md and llms.txt co-located with package.json at root is unconventional; consider relocating them to a .sourcevision/ subdirectory to match the tool's own output convention.
- context.md and llms.txt in package-root are sourcevision output artifacts — when sourcevision is run on the rex package itself, these files enter the zone graph, creating a self-referential loop where analysis output is analyzed. This inflates the package-root zone file count and contaminates the architectural graph with tool artifacts distinct from any source file.
- context.md and llms.txt are sourcevision output files appearing in the rex package-root zone. Add them to the sourcevision ignore list for the rex package to prevent self-referential zone contamination — tool output should not be indistinguishable from source files in the zone graph.

</insights>
